title: camelina sativa subsp. alyssum
intro: Camelina sativa subsp. alyssum, also known as a subspecies of the Camelina sativa plant, is a flowering plant belonging to the Brassicaceae family. It is a wild mustard relative, known for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of omega-3 fatty acids, as well as its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Camelina sativa subsp. alyssum include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its possible role in supporting brain function and cognitive health. Herbal preparations of Camelina sativa subsp. alyssum include oil extracts, capsules, and tinctures, which are often used to support overall well-being and provide relief from various health issues.
